question: When was John's eldest son crowned?
text:
John, King of England. John's first wife, Isabel, Countess of Gloucester, was released from imprisonment in 1214; she remarried twice, and died in 1217. John's second wife, Isabella of Angoulême, left England for Angoulême soon after the king's death; she became a powerful regional leader, but largely abandoned the children she had had by John. John had five legitimate children, all by Isabella. His eldest son, Henry III, ruled as king for the majority of the 13th century. Richard became a noted European leader and ultimately the King of the Romans in the Holy Roman Empire. Joan married Alexander II of Scotland to become his queen consort. Isabella married the Holy Roman Emperor Frederick II. His youngest daughter, Eleanor, married William Marshal's son, also called William, and later the famous English rebel Simon de Montfort. John had a number of illegitimate children by various mistresses, including nine sons – Richard, Oliver, John, Geoffrey, Henry, Osbert Gifford, Eudes, Bartholomew and probably Philip – and three daughters – Joan, Maud and probably Isabel. Of these, Joan became the most famous, marrying Prince Llywelyn the Great of Wales.

Westminster Abbey. Since the coronations in 1066 of both King Harold and William the Conqueror, coronations of English and British monarchs were held in the abbey. In 1216, Henry III was unable to be crowned in London when he first came to the throne, because the French prince Louis had taken control of the city, and so the king was crowned in Gloucester Cathedral. This coronation was deemed by the Pope to be improper, and a further coronation was held in the abbey on 17 May 1220. The Archbishop of Canterbury is the traditional cleric in the coronation ceremony.[citation needed]

Southeast Asia. The climate in Southeast Asia is mainly tropical–hot and humid all year round with plentiful rainfall. The rain forest is the second largest on earth (with the Amazon being the largest). An exception to this type of climate and vegetation is the mountain areas in the northern region, where high altitudes lead to milder temperatures and drier landscape. Other parts fall out of this climate because they are desert like.

Southeast Asia. Islam is the most widely practised religion in Southeast Asia, numbering approximately 240 million adherents which translate to about 40% of the entire population, with majorities in Indonesia, Brunei, Malaysia and in Southern Philippines with Indonesia as the largest and most populated Muslim country around the world. Countries in Southeast Asia practice many different religions. Buddhism is predominant in Thailand, Cambodia, Laos, Burma, Vietnam and Singapore. Ancestor worship and Confucianism are also widely practised in Vietnam and Singapore. Christianity is predominant in the Philippines, eastern Indonesia, East Malaysia and East Timor. The Philippines has the largest Roman Catholic population in Asia. East Timor is also predominantly Roman Catholic due to a history of Portuguese rule.

Zhejiang. Islam arrived 1,400 years ago in Zhejiang. Today Islam is practiced by a small number of people including virtually all the Hui Chinese living in Zhejiang. Another religion present in the province is She shamanism (practiced by She ethnic minority).

question: How many times did plague occur in the place of death of the creator of Violante?
text:
Violante (Titian). Violante is an oil painting attributed to Titian, dated to around 1515 and now held at the Kunsthistorisches Museum in Vienna.

Black Death. In 1466, perhaps 40,000 people died of the plague in Paris. During the 16th and 17th centuries, the plague was present in Paris around 30 per cent of the time. The Black Death ravaged Europe for three years before it continued on into Russia, where the disease was present somewhere in the country 25 times between 1350 to 1490. Plague epidemics ravaged London in 1563, 1593, 1603, 1625, 1636, and 1665, reducing its population by 10 to 30% during those years. Over 10% of Amsterdam's population died in 1623–25, and again in 1635–36, 1655, and 1664. Plague occurred in Venice 22 times between 1361 and 1528. The plague of 1576–77 killed 50,000 in Venice, almost a third of the population. Late outbreaks in central Europe included the Italian Plague of 1629–1631, which is associated with troop movements during the Thirty Years' War, and the Great Plague of Vienna in 1679. Over 60% of Norway's population died in 1348–50. The last plague outbreak ravaged Oslo in 1654.

The Martyrdom of Saint Lawrence (Titian). The Martyrdom of Saint Lawrence is a 1558 painting by Titian, now in the church of I Gesuiti in Venice. It so impressed Philip II of Spain that he commissioned a second version in 1567 for the basilica at El Escorial.

Initially, in the inaugural season when they joined the AL, the team was known as the Baltimore Orioles (unrelated to the contemporary Baltimore Orioles). Frank Farrell and Bill Devery acquired the dormant franchise, relocating it to New York City and christening it the New York Highlanders. Subsequently, the Highlanders were formally renamed the Yankees in 1903.

In the National League, the St. Louis Cardinals have featured in 19 World Series, achieving victory in 11. The New York / San Francisco Giants have competed in 20 World Series, securing 8 victories. The Brooklyn / Los Angeles Dodgers have appeared in 19 World Series, winning 6. Lastly, the Cincinnati Reds have participated in 9 World Series, claiming victory in 5.

Significantly, this was the first All-Star Game since 2002 where the outcome did not influence the World Series home-field advantage; instead, the team with the superior regular-season record will host. The Marlins were designated as the hosts by Major League Baseball Commissioner Rob Manfred on February 10, 2015, marking their inaugural All-Star Game hosting experience and leaving the Tampa Bay Rays as the sole MLB franchise yet to host an All-Star Game.

Major League Baseball Most Valuable Player Award: The MVP voting process takes place prior to the postseason, with the results unveiled post-World Series. The Baseball Writers' Association of America (BBWAA) originally involved three writers from each league city in 1938 for voting purposes, reducing this number to two per league city in 1961. Notably, the BBWAA refrains from providing a definitive interpretation of the term "most valuable," instead entrusting individual voters with the discretion to make this judgment based on their own criteria.

As a key fixture in Major League Baseball (MLB), the Yankees are a member club of the American League (AL) East division. They share the New York City baseball spotlight with the National League (NL)'s New York Mets, establishing a rivalry and shared tradition in the city. Initially, the Yankees franchise began as the Baltimore Orioles in the AL. The Orioles ceased operations, and in 1901, they were purchased by Frank Farrell and Bill Devery, subsequently relocating to New York City. Upon their move, the team was rebranded as the New York Highlanders in 1903, adopting the name New York Yankees later that same year.

In the context of the World Series, the Yankees possess an illustrious history. They have played in 40 World Series, securing an impressive 27 victories. This includes the distinction of being the first club to win back-to-back titles in the modern era. Other teams have had varying degrees of success in the World Series: the Philadelphia / Kansas City / Oakland Athletics have made 14 appearances and won 9 times, the Boston Red Sox have participated 12 times and won 8, including the inaugural World Series. On the National League side, the St. Louis Cardinals have appeared in 19 World Series, claiming 11 victories, the New York / San Francisco Giants have played in 20 and won 8, the Brooklyn / Los Angeles Dodgers have made 19 appearances and secured 6 victories, and the Cincinnati Reds have made 9 appearances and secured 5 wins.

The 2017 Major League Baseball All-Star Game marked the 88th edition of this prestigious event. It was held at Marlins Park in Miami, Florida, on July 11, 2017. This was the first All-Star Game since 2002 where the outcome did not determine home-field advantage for the World Series. Instead, the team with the better regular-season record would enjoy home-field advantage in the World Series. The game was televised nationally by Fox. The Miami Marlins were announced as hosts on February 10, 2015, by Major League Baseball Commissioner Rob Manfred, marking the first time a Miami-based team hosted the event and leaving the Tampa Bay Rays as the only MLB franchise without an All-Star Game hosting experience.

The Major League Baseball Most Valuable Player (MVP) Award is voted on by the Baseball Writers' Association of America (BBWAA) before the postseason but is not disclosed until after the conclusion of the World Series. Initially, in 1938, the BBWAA polled three writers from each league city, which was later reduced to two per league city in 1961. The BBWAA does not provide a clear definition of "most valuable," allowing individual voters to use their discretion when casting their ballots. This leaves room for interpretation based on various factors like team performance, individual statistics, and overall contributions to the team's success. This approach ensures that the award remains reflective of the varied criteria that voters may consider when evaluating a player's value to their team.
